summary: Markets exhibit cautious optimism with equities and Bitcoin grinding higher amid risk-on sentiment, while gold pulls back due to reduced safe-haven demand. Forex pairs like USD/JPY show bullish setups on breakouts, and Brent Crude eyes upside only on geopolitical surprises. Crypto tracks broader equity tone positively, with minor daily gains in BTC and ETH.

signals.name: Bitcoin
signals.reason: Critical level highlighted as potential trap or bull run, urging caution on directional bias.

signals.name: Ethereum
signals.reason: Ethereum edged higher 0.61% to $2,329.76 today, tracking the broader risk-on tone in equities and crypto sentiment.[1]

signals.name: Gold
signals.reason: Gold pulls back toward $4,580–$4,620 as risk appetite reduces safe-haven demand; silver also fell significantly with $80 resistance and $70 floor.[3][4]

signals.name: Brent Crude Oil
signals.reason: Long Brent Crude Futures recommended if daily close above $112.50, though extremely unlikely without surprise war resumption; crypto sentiment driven by EIA crude oil updates.[1][2]

signals.name: USD/JPY
signals.reason: Best trade as long USD/JPY following a daily New York close above ¥160 amid balanced optimism in FX markets.[2]

signals.name: S&P 500
signals.reason: S&P presses toward 7,300 and long recommended on daily close above 7,165, balancing optimism with geopolitics.[2][4]

signals.name: NASDAQ
signals.reason: Nasdaq targets 25,200–25,500 and long on daily close above 27,303, with record highs in tech like Intel.[2][4]

signals.name: Apple
signals.reason: No specific sentiment today; tracks broader NASDAQ bullish tone amid risk-on equities.[1][4]
signals.source: Crypto Market Pulse, Capital Street FX

signals.name: Nvidia
signals.reason: No direct mentions today; aligns with NASDAQ targets and tech strength in indices.[2][4]

signals.name: Solana
signals.reason: No specific discussion today; follows general crypto risk-on sentiment driven by equities.[1]

signals.name: USD/CAD
signals.reason: US dollar found support at 200-week EMA against CAD, forming a hammer and turning higher.[3]
